content:
# Transfer Limits FAQ

## External Transfer Limit
Northstar Bank applies a daily limit of 25000 to external transfers. Use this figure when a customer asks how much they can send externally in one day or why a same-day request cannot be accepted. The limit is daily, so multiple external transfer requests may be reviewed together against the same daily ceiling.

See "Transfer Types Fees And Limits Guide" for product-level transfer attributes and any related fee or limit context.

## Why A Transfer May Be Declined
When more than one issue applies to a submitted transfer, the denial reason is selected in this order: identity_not_verified, account_not_active, per_transfer_cap_exceeded, external_daily_limit_exceeded, insufficient_available_balance. Do not choose a later reason if an earlier reason in the sequence is already true.

Example: if the customer has enough available balance but the transfer would exceed the daily external limit, the response should cite the daily-limit issue, not balance availability. If identity verification has not been completed, that reason takes priority over account status, caps, daily limits, or balance.

## Customer Answers
Explain limits directly and avoid implying that support can bypass them. If the customer wants to retry, first identify which denial condition applies and whether a different amount or timing would address only that condition. For agent steps after a customer asks to place or resubmit a transfer, use "Internal: Transfer Submission Procedure" rather than giving procedural details from this FAQ.
title: Transfer Limits FAQ
